title: 5-Day Tokyo Cultural & Historical Journey
summary: An enriching mid-range journey through Tokyo's layered history and living culture. Balances iconic landmarks with quiet neighbourhood streets and museum visits, leaving room for spontaneous discovery each afternoon.

activities.title: Senso-ji Temple
activities.notes: Japan's oldest temple. Arrive early to experience morning rituals before the crowds. Walk the Nakamise shopping street for traditional snacks.

activities.title: Tokyo National Museum
activities.notes: Largest collection of Japanese art and artefacts in the country. Allow at least two hours for the main Honkan building.

activities.title: Dinner at Ueno Yabu Soba
activities.notes: Historic soba restaurant operating since 1892. Order the cold zaru soba with a side of kakiage tempura.

activities.title: Imperial Palace East Gardens
activities.notes: Free public garden on former castle grounds. Seasonal flowers make morning light exceptional for photography.

activities.title: Intermediatheque Museum
activities.notes: Free academic museum inside the old post office. Eclectic collection spanning natural history, architecture, and design.

activities.title: Nihonbashi Bridge & surrounding mercantile streets
activities.notes: Tokyo's historic commercial heart. Explore department stores that have operated for over a century.

activities.title: Yanaka Cemetery morning walk
activities.notes: Tokyo's best-preserved Edo-period neighbourhood. The cemetery is serene and reveals centuries of local history.

activities.title: Lunch at Yanaka Ginza shopping street
activities.notes: Traditional shotengai with indie food stalls. Try menchi katsu (fried minced-meat cutlet) from a street vendor.

activities.title: Nezu Shrine
activities.notes: One of Tokyo's oldest shrines with a tunnel of torii gates rivalling Fushimi Inari. Far fewer tourists.

activities.title: Akihabara Electric Town exploration
activities.notes: Walk the main street and side alleys. Visit the retro video-game floors of Yodobashi Camera.

activities.title: Curry lunch at Go Go Curry
activities.notes: Cult Kanazawa-style curry chain. Order the Katsu curry with large rice.

activities.title: teamLab Borderless
activities.notes: Book timed entry online. The largest digital art installation in the world. Allow 2–3 hours minimum.

activities.title: Meiji Jingu Shrine
activities.notes: Serene forested shrine dedicated to Emperor Meiji. Morning is quietest. Walk the entire forested approach.

activities.title: Takeshita Street lunch & Harajuku browsing
activities.notes: Grab a crepe from Marion Crepes (operating since 1977) and explore the side streets off Omotesando.

activities.title: Golden Gai farewell drinks
activities.notes: Maze of narrow alleys with tiny bars, each fitting 5–8 people. Pick any bar showing a welcoming sign — most welcome foreigners.

title: 3-Day Budget Paris Food & Markets Tour
summary: A relaxed, budget-conscious exploration of Paris through its street markets, boulangeries, and neighbourhood bistros. Prioritises slow mornings, picnics, and the free pleasures of the city over paid attractions.

activities.title: Rue Mouffetard market morning
activities.notes: One of Paris's oldest market streets. Busy Tuesday–Sunday. Pick up fresh bread, cheese, and fruit for a later picnic.

activities.title: Picnic in the Jardin des Plantes
activities.notes: Free botanical garden next to the market. Quiet benches and sprawling lawns. Perfect for unpacking your market haul.

activities.title: Café crème at a classic zinc bar
activities.notes: Pull up a stool at a traditional zinc counter — avoid tourist-facing terraces. Order standing for the lowest price.

activities.title: Canal Saint-Martin stroll & brunch
activities.notes: Walk the tree-lined canal and stop for brunch at one of the affordable neighbourhood cafés. Du Pain et des Idées bakery is nearby.

activities.title: Belleville Brûlerie coffee roasters
activities.notes: Celebrated Parisian roastery. Buy beans or sit for a pour-over. The neighbourhood is multicultural — explore the Vietnamese and Chinese restaurants nearby.

activities.title: Marché Belleville (Tuesday/Friday only)
activities.notes: Paris's most diverse outdoor market. Extraordinary range of North African and Asian produce. Arrive before 6 PM.

activities.title: Marché d'Aligre
activities.notes: Locals-only feel. Covered Beauvau market hall plus a sprawling outdoor flea market. Cheapest fresh produce in Paris.

activities.title: Lunch at Café de l'Industrie
activities.notes: Classic Parisian brasserie with a two-course lunch formule. Book or arrive at noon sharp to avoid a wait.

activities.title: Promenade Plantée (Coulée Verte)
activities.notes: World's first elevated park, predating New York's High Line. Free to walk. 4.5 km of gardens above a former railway viaduct.

title: 7-Day Cape Town Luxury Nature & Adventure
summary: An intensive luxury expedition across Cape Town's dramatic landscapes — from Table Mountain abseiling and Cape Point drives to shark cage diving and sunset whale-watching. Every day is an adventure.

activities.title: Table Mountain sunrise hike (Platteklip Gorge)
activities.notes: Start before sunrise to reach the summit for first light. The 1.5 km gorge trail takes 1.5–2 hours. Book a guide for safety.

activities.title: Table Mountain Abseiling
activities.notes: Abseil Africa operates 112 m descents off the back face. One of the world's highest commercial abseils. Book same day.

activities.title: Dinner at The Test Kitchen
activities.notes: Cape Town's most celebrated restaurant. Book months in advance. The 'Dark Room' tasting menu is exceptional.

activities.title: Chapman's Peak Drive
activities.notes: One of the world's most scenic coastal drives. 9 km of cliff-hugging road with 114 curves. Stop at every viewpoint.

activities.title: Cape Point & Cape of Good Hope
activities.notes: Hike to the lighthouse for the most southwesterly point of Africa. Watch for Cape mountain zebra and ostriches.

activities.title: Boulder's Beach penguin colony
activities.notes: African penguin colony of over 3,000 birds. Book timed entry. The snorkelling section is remarkable.

activities.title: Stellenbosch mountain biking
activities.notes: Hire a full-suspension bike from Bikeabout. The Jonkershoek Valley trail is technically challenging with exceptional views.

activities.title: Lunch at Jordan Restaurant
activities.notes: Estate restaurant with panoramic views over Stellenbosch valley. The six-course tasting menu pairs each course with estate wine.

activities.title: Franschhoek wine tram & valley sunset
activities.notes: Hop-on-hop-off tram touring the valley's top estates. Book the late afternoon loop for golden-hour vineyard light.

activities.title: Great White Shark cage diving
activities.notes: 2.5-hour drive from Cape Town. Marine Dynamics is the most ethical operator. No diving experience needed. Sea conditions permitting.

activities.title: Walker Bay whale watching (seasonal Jun–Nov)
activities.notes: Best land-based whale watching in the world. Southern right whales calve in the bay. Book a boat tour for closer encounters.

activities.title: Dinner at Harbour House Hermanus
activities.notes: Perched above the old harbour with Atlantic views. Order the West Coast line-fish caught that morning.

activities.title: Kitesurfing lesson at Bloubergstrand
activities.notes: Cape Town is among the world's top kitesurfing destinations. Capricorn Kitesurfing offers 3-hour beginner lessons with all equipment.

activities.title: Lunch at The Boathouse Bloubergstrand
activities.notes: Watch kite-surfers while eating fish and chips with a direct Table Mountain view across the bay.

activities.title: V&A Waterfront sunset & cocktails
activities.notes: Sunset from the harbour is spectacular with Table Mountain as backdrop. Seapoint Pavilion nearby for a post-swim drink.

activities.title: Bo-Kaap neighbourhood walk
activities.notes: Cape Malay heritage neighbourhood with brightly painted houses. Book a guided walking tour to understand the community's history.

activities.title: Lunch at Biesmiellah Restaurant
activities.notes: Legendary Cape Malay restaurant in operation since 1960. Order the denningvleis (tamarind lamb) and koesisters for dessert.

activities.title: Norval Foundation & art walk
activities.notes: World-class contemporary art museum with sculpture gardens. The permanent collection focuses on South African artists.

activities.title: Tandem paragliding off Signal Hill
activities.notes: Fly with Para-Pax over the city bowl, sea point, and Camps Bay. The 10–15 minute flight lands on the beach. Book early morning for calm winds.

activities.title: Brunch at Café Caprice
activities.notes: Cape Town institution on the Camps Bay beach strip. Outdoor terrace with white-sand beach views. Order the eggs benedict.

activities.title: Sunset dinner at La Colombe
activities.notes: Consistently ranked among Africa's top ten restaurants. The 'World in a Can' tasting menu is the signature dish. Essential booking.
